prompt:
Scene:
A square commercial presentation with realistic materials, clean staging, and believable product photography or retail lighting. Build the frame around a disciplined family of variants presented as one product language. Keep the lighting, camera distance, and spatial organization tuned for a high-signal research image rather than a casual snapshot. The image belongs to the Brand Identity Mockup Set theme and should feel immediately legible as a prompt atlas theme for brand identity mockup set, testing text-rendering, structured-layout, commercial-mockup with gpt image 2..

Subject:
Center the study on a disciplined family of variants presented as one product language. Translate the theme into a concrete visual statement instead of an abstract mood board: the viewer should understand what kind of image this is, what is being tested, and why the composition is difficult. Use the slug idea of brand identity mockup set as a literal guide for subject matter, materials, and staging. If helpful, borrow the broad visual cues suggested by logo, but keep the result original and specific to this study.

Important details:
The mockup should feel saleable and design-ready: sharp edges, controlled reflections, credible paper or plastic behavior, and precise placement. Preserve a disciplined square composition, clear focal hierarchy, and enough secondary information to make the image feel complete rather than underspecified. The image should look polished, novel, and exhibition-ready, with topic-specific credibility coming from the way surfaces, structure, and visual language reinforce Brand Identity Mockup Set. Avoid generic filler elements that do not serve the main concept.

Use case:
Brand Identity Mockup Set single study 2/8, testing text-rendering, structured-layout, commercial-mockup through the variant grid variation.

Constraints:
No readable text, no watermark, no logos, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio, no real brand names, no cheap stock-photo styling, no broken perspective, no watermark, no accidental readable packaging copy, no cluttered retail shelf unless intended.
